line: - Registered on Agent Mail as `CyanEagle`, checked inbox, coordinated with other agents, acknowledged follow-up, and released reservations.
- embedding-backed similarity can beat naive compact-text overlap on a realistic release/deploy query,
- reranking can change final ordering and leaves inspectable rerank metadata/reasons,
- planner/cache diagnostics expose semantic stages, embedding/ranking invalidation, and degraded fallback behavior.
- Existing retrieval/result test surfaces were heavily reserved by other agents; isolating proof coverage into a dedicated test file avoided communication purgatory and edit collisions.

items: Existing retrieval/result test surfaces were heavily reserved by other agents; isolating proof coverage into a dedicated test file avoided communication purgatory and edit collisions.

line: - Pack-level rerank metadata is intentionally suppressed when retained items disagree, so proof coverage must assert item-level rerank influence in mixed-result scenarios.

items: Pack-level rerank metadata is intentionally suppressed when retained items disagree, so proof coverage must assert item-level rerank influence in mixed-result scenarios.

line: - Used a new isolated core integration test file instead of contested files like `crates/membrain-core/tests/recall_pipeline_integration.rs`

items: Used a new isolated core integration test file instead of contested files like `crates/membrain-core/tests/recall_pipeline_integration.rs`

line: - Proved the acceptance criteria through public planner/ranking/embed/cache/result APIs rather than editing shared retrieval internals

items: Proved the acceptance criteria through public planner/ranking/embed/cache/result APIs rather than editing shared retrieval internals

line: - The proof is currently concentrated in core integration coverage; CLI/daemon-specific presentation of the same diagnostics is still separate work if broader parity evidence is desired.

items: The proof is currently concentrated in core integration coverage; CLI/daemon-specific presentation of the same diagnostics is still separate work if broader parity evidence is desired.
